description:
This is how I unlocked Claude Code's secret agent swarm feature that rivals Kimi K2.5's advanced agent swarm system. In this video, I explore Matt Kelly's discovery of a feature flag buried in Claude Code's JavaScript that enables dynamic agent swarming, where specialized subagents are created automatically from scratch, and can work in parallel on complex tasks. We'll dive into what makes this feature awesome, and talk about how it compares to Kimi 2.5's implementation. Did Anthropic steal the idea? And why are they hiding it?

🔗 Relevant Links
Mike's Tweet - https://x.com/NicerInPerson/status/2014989679796347375
Claude Sneak Peak - https://github.com/mikekelly/claude-sneakpeek
Video from AI Jason - https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=LCYBVpSB0Wo
Agents Repo - https://github.com/wshobson/agents
BMAD - https://github.com/bmad-code-org/BMAD-METHOD
